rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
Students will have frequent opportunities to communicate with their classmates in English in spontaneous, prepared, and mixed settings in order to develop practical communication skills.
The semester will introduce four “speech styles,” a thematic topic which presents an advanced skill for communication. The topic will be presented in the form of a short lecture and vocabulary bundle. Students will practice the speech style in pairs/groups and will be assessed through short spoken responses (participation score) and longer prepared speaking tasks (midterm and final assessments).
aims:
This is a speaking‑focused English language course. This class builds upon the foundational oral communication skills learned in earlier courses such as Eikaiwa (skills: casual conversation techniques and expressions) and move toward more advanced communication styles and skills.
The aim for this course is that it will equip you with real-world communication skills for you to go out and get along with your international peers. However, even if you don’t plan on going abroad, the knowledge you gain in the course will be valuable for you to understand the “pointy end” of expression in English, and will be essential for your development as an educator, etc.
goals: use advanced speaking styles as part of your own communication, i.e., your own “voice”
present your opinion and answer questions after
listen to another speaker and present an argument against their topic

prereq:
‑ class size is limited to 24 students (enrollment preference will be given to 3rd and 4th year students and/or those working
toward their teaching license).
prep:
Preview and review are essential parts of the long-term memory formation necessary for language learning.
Information will be provided in class.
